title: Lens Effects: Chromatic Aberration, Vignette, Film Grain
description: Implements lens chromatic aberration (channel-offset UV sample), vignette (radial exposure falloff approximating real lens darkening), and film grain (dynamic noise with per-channel intensity calibrated to match photographic grain statistics) as physically inspired effects that ground the rendered image in a camera-captured aesthetic. These effects are subtle in calibrated form but become noticeable artifacts when over-applied; the lesson emphasizes tasteful implementation ranges used by professional cinematic DPs. Students will implement all three effects, calibrate grain intensity to match a provided photographic reference, and produce a before/after comparison demonstrating the subtle versus over-applied settings.
